segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Hello?
segments.zh: 喂？

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Oh hello.
segments.zh: 哦你好。我叫简。请问您是负责巴克沃斯保护小组的人吗？

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: My name's Jan.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Are you the right person to talk to about the Buckworth Conservation Group?

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Yes, I'm Peter.
segments.zh: 是的，我是彼得，我是秘书。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: I'm the secretary.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Good.
segments.zh: 好的。我刚搬到这个地区，有兴趣参与进来。我以前住的地方也参加过类似的小组。能给我讲讲你们的活动吗？

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I've just moved to this area, and I'm interested in getting involved.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I was in a similar group where I used to live.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Could you tell me something about your activities, please?

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Of course.
segments.zh: 当然。我们既有常规活动也有特别活动。常规活动之一是保持海滩没有垃圾。我们几个人每个月花几个小时做这件事，要清理的垃圾多得吓人。我真希望人们能更负责任，把垃圾带回家。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Well, we have a mixture of regular activities and special events.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: One of the regular ones is trying to keep the beach free of litter.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: A few of us spend a couple of hours a month on it, and it's awful how much there is to clear.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: I wish people would be more responsible and take it home with them.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I totally agree.
segments.zh: 我完全同意。我很乐意帮忙。可以带狗吗？

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I'd be happy to help with that.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Is it OK to take dogs?

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: I'm afraid not, as they're banned from the beach itself.
segments.zh: 恐怕不行，因为海滩本身禁止狗进入。不过你可以带它们沿着悬崖走。也欢迎带孩子来。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: You can take them along the cliffs, though.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: And children are welcome.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: We also manage a nature reserve, and there's a lot to do there all year round.
segments.zh: 我们还管理一个自然保护区，那里一年四季都有很多事情要做。比如，因为那里是热门景点，我们花很多时间维护步道，确保它们适合行走。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: For example, because it's a popular place to visit, we spend a lot of time looking after the paths and making sure they're in good condition for walking.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I could certainly help with that.
segments.zh: 我肯定能帮上忙。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Good.
segments.zh: 很好。我们还有一个创建新栖息地的项目。我们刚做完并安装了供鸟类使用的巢箱，接下来要着手吸引昆虫——它们对保护区的生物多样性很重要。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: And we have a programme of creating new habitats there.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: We've just finished making and installing nesting boxes for birds to use, and next we're going to work on encouraging insects – they're important for the biodiversity of the reserve.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: They certainly are.
segments.zh: 确实如此。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Oh, and we're also running a project to identify the different species of butterflies that visit the reserve.
segments.zh: 哦，我们还在进行一个项目，识别到访保护区的不同蝴蝶种类。你可能会对参与这个感兴趣。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: You might be interested in taking part in that.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。我以前住的地方参与过类似的项目，统计所有飞蛾的种类。我会喜欢的。

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I was involved in something similar where I used to live, counting all the species of moths.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I'd enjoy that.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Another job we're doing at the reserve is replacing the wall on the southern side, between the parking area and our woodshed.
segments.zh: 我们在保护区的另一项工作是更换南侧的墙，就在停车场和木柴棚之间。上个月一场暴风雨把它严重损坏了。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: It was badly damaged in a storm last month.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Then as I said, we have a programme of events as well, both at the weekend, and during the week.
segments.zh: 然后像我说的，我们还有一系列活动，周末和工作日都有。

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。我想你们有导游徒步吧？我刚来这个地区，想了解一下当地的乡村。

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I presume you have guided walks?

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: I'd like to get to know the local countryside, as I'm new to the area.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: Yes, we do.
segments.zh: 是的，我们有。下一次徒步是去拉斯顿岛，下下周六。我们将在低潮时在邓斯莫尔海滩的停车场集合——那时沙子足够干，我们可以走到岛上而不会弄湿脚。

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: The next walk is to Ruston Island, a week on Saturday.

segments.speaker: PETER
segments.en: We'll be meeting in the car park at Dunsmore Beach at low tide – that's when the sands are dry enough for us to walk to the island without getting wet.

segments.speaker: JAN
segments.en: Sounds good.
segments.zh: 听起来不错。

items.prompt: making sure the beach does not have ________ on it

pairs.note: 题干用否定结构，原文用'free of'表达相同意思

paraphrase.explain: 定位句说'keep the beach free of litter'，与题干'does not have litter'同义，因此答案就是litter。

pairs.note: 题干'no'对应原文中疑问句'Is it OK to take'，表示否定含义，即不允许带狗。
答案'dogs'在原文中直接出现，是原词。

paraphrase.explain: 通过疑问句'Is it OK to take dogs?'中的否定含义定位到'no dogs'，答案直接是'dogs'。

items.prompt: next task is taking action to attract ________ to the place

pairs.note: attract 和 encouraging 都表示'吸引'，但原文用 encouraging 更强调主动促进。
to the place 对应 there，指代前文提到的保护区。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'next we're going to work on encouraging insects'，其中 encouraging 对应题干 attract，insects 就是答案。

pairs.note: 题干中的'types of'被替换为'different species of'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句中的'identify the different species of butterflies'与题干'identifying types of butterflies'对应，'different species of'替换了'types of'。

pairs.note: 题干说'建造新的'，原文说'替换'，意思相近，都是指建一个新东西。
答案和原文完全一致，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问建造一个新的什么，原文说替换南边的墙，所以答案就是wall。

items.prompt: walk across the sands and reach the ________

pairs.note: 原文用'walk to the island'替换了题干中的'walk across the sands and reach the'，但意思相同。
原文直接出现'to the island'，与题干'reach the'对应，答案就是island。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'walk to the island'，其中'to the island'直接对应题干中的'reach the'，因此答案就是island。
